questions.q: In the Ramayana, what is Rama Setu?
options: A sacred bow given to Rama
A bridge built across the sea to Lanka
questions.explanation: Rama Setu is the great causeway built across the ocean so Rama's army could cross to Lanka.

questions.q: Whose army builds the Rama Setu in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The vanara army, allied with Rama, constructs the bridge across the sea.

questions.q: To which island does the Rama Setu lead in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The bridge spans the ocean to reach Lanka, the kingdom of Ravana.

questions.q: Why does Rama lead his army across the Rama Setu to Lanka in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama crosses to Lanka to rescue Sita, who had been abducted by Ravana.

questions.q: Who is the demon king of Lanka whom Rama seeks to confront after crossing the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka, had abducted Sita and is the foe Rama marches against.

questions.q: In which epic is the building of the Rama Setu narrated?
questions.explanation: The construction of the bridge is told in the Ramayana, in its Yuddha Kanda.

questions.q: What did the vanaras chiefly use to construct the Rama Setu in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The vanaras gathered great stones, rocks, and trees to raise the causeway across the sea.

questions.q: Whose name is said to be inscribed on the floating stones of the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: In the tradition, the stones bearing Rama's name floated and formed the bridge.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the Rama Setu crosses what kind of body of water?
questions.explanation: The bridge is built across the ocean separating the mainland from Lanka.

questions.q: Which devoted vanara warrior, who first leapt to Lanka, fights alongside Rama after the Rama Setu is built?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the great vanara devotee, had already leapt to Lanka and aids Rama in the war.

questions.q: Who is the vanara architect said to direct the building of the Rama Setu in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Nala, son of the divine architect Vishvakarma, directs the construction of the bridge.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of the Ramayana is the building of the Rama Setu described?
questions.explanation: The bridge is built in the Yuddha Kanda, the book of the war against Ravana.

questions.q: Which ocean-god does Rama appeal to for safe passage before the Rama Setu is built?
questions.explanation: Rama entreats Varuna, the god of the ocean and waters, who grants safe passage for the bridge.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, how does Rama finally compel the ocean to allow the Rama Setu to be built?
options: By taking up his bow and threatening to dry the sea
By offering a great sacrifice of gold
By fasting for a hundred years
By summoning Hanuman to drink the sea
questions.explanation: After Rama prepares to loose a fiery arrow that could dry the waters, the ocean-god appears and grants passage.

questions.q: The vanara Nala, who builds the Rama Setu, is the son of which divine architect of the gods?
questions.explanation: Nala is the son of Vishvakarma, the divine architect of the gods, and inherits his skill in construction.

questions.q: Which vanara king, Hanuman's lord and Rama's ally, commands the army that builds the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Sugriva, the vanara king of Kishkindha, leads the army allied with Rama in the campaign.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is Rama's loyal brother who accompanies him across the Rama Setu to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, Rama's devoted brother, shares his exile and crosses to Lanka with him.

questions.q: Which righteous brother of Ravana defects to Rama and crosses with the army after the Rama Setu is built?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, the virtuous brother of Ravana, joins Rama and is later crowned king of Lanka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, how many days are the vanaras said to have taken to build the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana relates that the great bridge was completed in five days.

questions.q: Of which divine being is Rama regarded as an avatara in the tradition surrounding the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Rama is revered as one of the principal avataras of Vishnu, descended to vanquish Ravana.

questions.q: By what other name is the Rama Setu commonly known in tradition?
questions.explanation: The bridge is also called Setu Bandha (Rameswaram) and is popularly known as Adam's Bridge.

questions.q: Near which sacred pilgrimage town associated with Rama is the Rama Setu traditionally said to begin?
questions.explanation: The bridge is traditionally said to begin near Rameswaram, a major pilgrimage site linked to Rama.

questions.q: How many yojanas long is the Rama Setu said to be in Valmiki's Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the bridge as one hundred yojanas long and ten yojanas wide.

questions.q: How wide is the Rama Setu said to be in Valmiki's Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana gives the bridge a width of ten yojanas, matching its hundred-yojana length.

questions.q: Which fellow vanara, often paired with Nala in the lore, is also credited with helping construct the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: The vanara Nila is, alongside Nala, associated with the building of the great bridge in the tradition.

questions.q: From which clan or lineage do the vanara bridge-builders Nala and Nila descend in the Ramayana?
options: Descendants of celestial craftsmen and fire
questions.explanation: Nala is born of Vishvakarma and Nila of Agni, lending them their craft and might for the work.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, how many days does Rama wait by the shore before the ocean-god responds about the Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Rama lies in supplication by the sea for three days before, unanswered, he takes up his bow.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, where does Varuna advise Rama to loose the arrow he had readied, rather than at the sea?
options: At the peak of Mount Meru
At a region of demons to the north
questions.explanation: Varuna asks Rama to direct the powerful arrow at the Drumakulya region to the north rather than dry the ocean.

questions.q: Which mighty vanara, son of Vayu, had already crossed to Lanka before the Rama Setu was ever built?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the son of the wind-god Vayu, leapt the ocean to Lanka in the Sundara Kanda, before the bridge was made.

questions.q: Within the Ramayana's pilgrimage tradition, the Rama Setu is linked to a famed jyotirlinga shrine of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Rameswaram shrine at the bridge's foot enshrines a jyotirlinga of Shiva, said to be installed by Rama himself.
